package.name: openral_safety_kernel
package.description:
ADR-0020 — C++ safety kernel. Separate process owning the chunk-rate safety boundary on the OpenRAL ROS 2 graph. Replaces F5's Python pass-through (packages/openral_safety/SafetyPassthroughNode) behind the same topic contract (CLAUDE.md §1.5: "Python proposes, C++ disposes").
Subscribes /openral/candidate_action, validates against the intersection of the robot ceiling (RobotDescription.safety) and the optional rSkill envelope, republishes the chunk verbatim on /openral/safe_action when validated, or drops + publishes FailureTrigger on /openral/failure/safety and /openral/estop on violation. Recovery via /openral/estop_reset (std_srvs/Trigger). Defense-in-depth: subscribes to /openral/estop itself so external estop sources latch the kernel.
Real-time contract: validator allocation-free; SCHED_FIFO best- effort; LTTng tracepoints openral:safety_check_{begin,end} correlated with the active OTel trace_id (PR #131 / F9).
